_doc: Each entry: folder_id -> {short_name, year, family, critiques_received, self_acknowledged_limits}. critiques_received are concise paraphrases of how later papers criticized this benchmark. Use as Limitation evidence when scoring.

14064.note: Theoretical impossibility result for fair risk scores; not a dataset/benchmark. Many fairness benchmarks (BBQ/CBBQ etc.) implicitly assume single fairness criterion which Kleinberg shows cannot coexist.
14064.self_acknowledged_limits: Limited to risk-score calibration setting; impossibility holds only when base rates differ across groups.

13459.note: Wikipedia-derived gendered ambiguous pronoun dataset (8908 pairs) for coreference; gender-balanced by construction.
critiques_received: Levy 2021 (BUG): Wikipedia text + ambiguous pronouns is artificial; lacks naturalistic occupational stereotype signal.
Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): binary gender only; ignores LGBTQ+ identities.
Vanmassenhove 2021 (NeuTral): treats gender as binary he/she, no neutral 'they' baseline.
13459.self_acknowledged_limits: English Wikipedia only; binary masculine/feminine pronouns; coreference task only.

10838.note: 120 hand-built templates × 60 occupations × {he/she/they}; minimal-pair Winograd-style coreference test, paired with US BLS occupational gender stats.
critiques_received: Levy 2021 (BUG): synthetic templates underestimate real-world bias; tiny size (720 sentences); cannot stress-test models.
Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): only binary gender + 'they'; no LGBTQ+ axis.
Vanmassenhove 2021 (NeuTral Rewriter): Winogender pairs forced into binary categorization, no neutral targets.
Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): occupation axis only, ignores hundreds of other demographic descriptors.
10838.self_acknowledged_limits: Diagnostic, not training; binary occupation set; synthetic templates.

13473.note: 3160 sentences in two evaluation types (Type1/Type2); occupation × pronoun pro-/anti-stereotype contrast, debiasing methods evaluated.
critiques_received: Levy 2021 (BUG): same as Winogender — synthetic, scale-limited.
Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): binary; LGBTQ+ identities absent.
Vanmassenhove 2021 (NeuTral): WinoBias+ extends with neutral pronouns; original WinoBias hard-codes binary gender.
Stanovsky 2019 et seq.: limited to occupational stereotypes from BLS; no race/age/disability axes.
13473.self_acknowledged_limits: Templates and 40 occupations; English; gender as he/she only.

13452.note: Probes static word embeddings + NLI for biased inferences across gender, race, religion; debias via projection.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): static embedding probes are decoupled from real downstream usage and dialog generation.
Dhamala 2021 (BOLD): bias measured intrinsically in embeddings does not transfer to generation behavior.
Blodgett 2021 (Stereotyping Norwegian Salmon, applies to many MLM/embedding probes): construct underspecified.
13452.self_acknowledged_limits: Focused on embedding-level intervention; downstream effect partial.

13451.note: Measures English vs German contextual gender stereotypes in BERT via cloze probing on occupation; proposes counterfactual data substitution debiasing.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): occupation-only, narrow; small descriptor set.
Vanmassenhove 2021: binary gender categories.
Generation-side benchmarks (BOLD, HONEST): cloze probing intrinsic, mismatched with generation harms.
13451.self_acknowledged_limits: EN/DE only; pronoun-occupation only; intrinsic measure.

13012.note: 45k Wikipedia sentences with gender labels for 4 relation types (spouse, hypernym, child, residence); evaluates gender bias in relation extraction systems.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): RE-only; dimensional coverage limited to 4 relations.
Wikipedia source skews male-biased; data inherits encyclopedic biases.
Felkner 2023: binary gender; LGBTQ+ erased.
13012.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note Wikipedia underrepresents women (~18% of biographies); 4 relations only.

11163.note: Underspecified QA framework; uses two-question reversibility to net out model positional bias; covers gender, nationality, ethnicity, religion.
critiques_received: Parrish 2022 (BBQ): UnQover's reversal control is good but ambiguous-context Qs lack a 'correct' answer; only measures relative preference, not absolute harm.
Jin 2024 (KoBBQ) & Huang 2024 (CBBQ): English-only; protected-class taxonomy follows US categories.
Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): 4 axes is too few.
11163.self_acknowledged_limits: Synthetic templates; subject-name based stereotypes; English.

13470.note: Same paper as 11163 (UnQover); will be deduplicated in scoring.

10856.note: 1508 pairs across 9 bias types collected via Amazon MTurk; pseudo-log-likelihood metric for MLMs.
critiques_received: Blodgett 2021: stereotyping content underspecified, many examples invalid; construct of 'social bias' not operationalized.
Nadeem 2021 (StereoSet) & later authors: PLL metric sensitive to spurious lexical differences.
Jin 2024 (KoBBQ): US-centric stereotypes; not transferable to other cultures.
Huang 2024 (CBBQ): no Chinese-context categories (region, hukou).
10856.self_acknowledged_limits: MTurk workers from US/UK; English; possibly noisy crowd judgements; nine fixed categories.

13031.note: Uses Reddit comments as real-world conversational data to evaluate and debias DialoGPT across religion, race, gender, queerness, orientation; counterfactual augmentation debiasing.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): Reddit demographics skewed (mostly young Western males) → coverage gap.
Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): queerness category exists but is shallow; community-in-the-loop missing.
Generation-bench critiques: PPL-style metric still intrinsic-leaning.
13031.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag Reddit demographic skew and English-only; perplexity-based metric still proxies bias rather than harm.

12997.note: Rule-based + neural rewriter from gendered to gender-neutral English; releases WinoBias+ (with neutral pronouns) for evaluation.
critiques_received: Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): neutral 'they' is one step but does not capture LGBTQ+ identities or stereotypes.
Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): one-axis (gender) rewriter does not cover other identities.
Pre-trained MT models perform poorly on neutral forms in many languages — extension to non-English limited.
12997.self_acknowledged_limits: English-only; rule-based fallback for uncovered constructions; evaluates rewriting quality, not downstream harm.

13455.note: 23,679 prompts spanning profession, gender, race, religion, political ideology; auto metrics for sentiment/toxicity/regard/psycholinguistic norms in open-ended generation.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): 5 axes & ~600 descriptors not covered; uses Wikipedia which propagates encyclopedic bias.
Nozza 2021 (HONEST): sentiment/regard metrics correlate weakly with hurtfulness; English-only.
Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): no LGBTQ+ subgroups.
13455.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag Wikipedia coverage bias and that automatic toxicity classifiers themselves contain bias.

13457.note: 108k naturalistic English sentences mined from corpora with stereotypical / anti-stereotypical / neutral labels for occupational coreference; large-scale alternative to Winogender/WinoBias.
critiques_received: Felkner 2023: binary gender; no LGBTQ+ extension.
Smith 2022: occupation-axis only.
Vanmassenhove 2021: binary pronoun mining.
13457.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 'gold' subset still small (~1.7k); naturalistic mining surfaces messy text; English only.

13019.note: 17k context-association triples (stereo / anti-stereo / unrelated) over gender, profession, race, religion; stereotype score (SS) + LM score combined.
critiques_received: Blodgett 2021 (Stereotyping Norwegian Salmon): MTurk-collected stereotypes are noisy, often invalid, construct unclear.
Nangia 2020 (CrowS-Pairs): SS metric distorted when sentences differ in fluency.
Smith 2022, Jin 2024, Huang 2024: 4 axes insufficient; English/US categories only.
13019.self_acknowledged_limits: MTurk crowdsourcing risk; only 4 stereotype domains; English.

13465.note: Templated cloze prompts in 6 languages; uses HurtLex categories to score hurtful completions for women vs men.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): only binary gender; few descriptors.
Felkner 2023: gender binary, no LGBTQ+; HurtLex coverage of slurs incomplete.
Authors of multilingual studies note HurtLex resource quality varies sharply across languages.
13465.self_acknowledged_limits: Reliance on HurtLex; binary gender; cloze format limits realism.

10858.note: Hand-built 58k question instances across 9 US-relevant social dimensions; ambiguous + disambiguated context pairs; bias score conditioned on context.
critiques_received: Jin 2024 (KoBBQ): explicitly critiques BBQ for English-only and US categories; ~70% of BBQ items unsuitable for Korean culture; introduces 5 new Korean dimensions.
Huang & Xiong 2024 (CBBQ): BBQ misses Chinese-relevant categories like region/hukou/educational background; only 9 dimensions vs 14 in CBBQ.
Smith 2022 (HolisticBias): BBQ's 9 axes are too coarse compared to 600+ HolisticBias descriptors.
10858.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note US-English social biases only; nine categories; relies on attested-stereotype literature.

13467.note: 98k human-perturbed text pairs across gender/race/age (Panda dataset); used to fine-tune a perturber model and improve fairness on multiple downstream tasks.
critiques_received: Smith 2022 (HolisticBias) — same group: Panda only 3 axes; HolisticBias extends to 13 axes.
Real-world deployment papers: perturbation may produce ungrammatical or culturally awkward outputs in low-resource settings.
Jin 2024: Panda is English-only; perturber not transferable.
13467.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag English-only and 3-axis scope; perturber accuracy ~92%, errors propagate.

13463.note: 459k sentence prompts × 600 descriptor terms × 13 demographic axes; participatory list assembled with affected communities; used for bias/toxicity in dialog generation.
critiques_received: Felkner 2023 (WinoQueer): even HolisticBias's LGBTQ+ axis is descriptor-list-based; community endorsement weaker than WinoQueer's iterative loop.
Jin 2024: still English/US-centric in descriptor sourcing.
Huang 2024 (CBBQ): cultural translation of HolisticBias to Chinese non-trivial; many descriptors not applicable.
13463.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note descriptors are English; some categories (caste, neurodiversity) under-validated; templates are short.

13008.note: Community-in-the-loop benchmark (45k sentences) for anti-LGBTQ+ bias in MLMs; co-designed with LGBTQ+ community members; covers 9 queer identities × 11 stereotype types.
critiques_received: Future surveys: still English-only; fluency artifacts in templates.
Generation-side critique: pseudo-log-likelihood metric still intrinsic, not deployment-grounded.
13008.self_acknowledged_limits: English-only; MLM PLL metric; sample of US-context stereotypes; community covers but cannot exhaust queer identity space.

12363.note: Spans toxicity / bias / value-alignment for conversational LLMs; uses Social Chemistry 101 norms + active and passive value-alignment tests.
critiques_received: Survey papers (e.g., TrustLLM): TrustGPT mixes axes loosely; metrics inconsistent across components.
HolisticBias / KoBBQ: bias dimension uses small fixed set; not multilingual.
Construct validity literature: 'value-alignment' construct underspecified in TrustGPT (no normative source mapping).
12363.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors call it a starting framework, expanding work; no formal documentation card; metrics for value-alignment are heuristic.

13461.note: 118 queries × pair of paragraphs (female/male) for gender representation bias in information retrieval; combines neutral and stereotyped queries.
critiques_received: HolisticBias / WinoQueer perspective: binary gender only; small (118).
IR community: relevance judgments are coarse; no LGBTQ+ extension.
13461.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag small scale (118 queries) and binary gender.

12853.note: 1767 moral scenarios (680 high-ambiguity / 687 low-ambiguity), 2 actions each; uses survey methodology to elicit moral choices, with question-format-entropy and Recall-of-Action metrics for 28 LLMs.
critiques_received: Within-moral-evaluation literature: 1767 scenarios can be memorized; lacks dynamic update.
Cross-cultural moral psychology: scenarios skewed toward Western moral intuitions (commonsense from English-speaking authors).
BBQ-family: not a fairness/bias benchmark per se; categorization in fairness folder is borderline.
12853.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors acknowledge dataset is English; rule labels (e.g., 'do not kill') are Western canonical; high-ambiguity items lack ground-truth right answer.

13029.note: Korean-localized BBQ; 76k samples over 12 categories incl. 5 Korea-specific (e.g., regional, military, marital). Filters BBQ items not applicable in Korean culture (~70%) and adds new categories.
critiques_received: Future surveys: still QA-only; not generation; LLM judge variance.
Coverage of Korean dialects/regions partial.
13029.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note ambiguous-context labels reflect Korean majority view; LLM-as-judge calibration limited.

8278.note: Chinese BBQ-style benchmark; 100k+ questions over 14 social dimensions including China-specific (region, hukou, educational background, marital status, appearance); 4-step human-AI collaborative curation.
critiques_received: Recent Chinese fairness surveys: AI-assisted generation may amplify model biases; manual review coverage uneven.
QA-only; does not test multi-turn or generation drift.
8278.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note AI-assistance may inject model bias; some categories (e.g., 户籍) hard to ground in legal sources.
